meta.summary: Sustainability review of all 19 backlog items. The finding both reviewers converged on: build the shared foundations FIRST, because four of the features are themselves platform primitives (F7=pre-signal hook, F11=fill capture, F17=run ledger, F18=state schema) and three others would each fork the code if built naively (F6/F15 sizing+cost, F3 attribution). Four prerequisite refactors (R1-R4) unify what is already diverging before we add to it. Foundations P0-A..P0-I are the architectural primitives; R1/R2/R3 are the concrete refactor tickets that realize C/B/A, R4 hardens the invariant-3 test. Every item is placed in exactly one of five dependency-ordered phases; validate.py enforces that nothing is dropped and dependencies flow forward.
meta.phase_order_note: Statistical validity (Phase 1) is sequenced before data integrity (Phase 2) on purpose: F1 (point-in-time data) is blocked on external vendor procurement, while the stats work is pure code and unblocked, so it proceeds in parallel with data acquisition. The Architect's counter (don't certify survivorship-biased data) is handled by the continuous-backlog rule: F2's DSR/PBO is re-run once F1 lands, and every synthetic/non-PIT result keeps its banner (invariant #5).

foundations.title: Volume/ADV in the data layer
foundations.why: data.py fetches only Close; F6 impact costs and F15 ADV caps both need real trailing volume. Build once with a versioned cache key so old Close-only parquet caches invalidate.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: F6 and F15 each fetch volume ad-hoc with divergent lookback/adjustment conventions.

foundations.title: Fill capture in execution + paper trade log
foundations.why: execution_ibkr.rebalance() calls placeOrder() and discards the result; no trade records decision price vs fill. F3, F10 and F11 all need realized fills.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: Three features invent three different Trade/fill schemas and AUD-conversion conventions.

foundations.title: One unified cost function across engines
foundations.why: backtest.py charges cost as turnover*rate; paper_trade.py inlines slippage into the fill price then adds commission+stamp separately. Two cost paths already diverge before F6.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: F6 adds a third divergent cost path, reintroducing the exact backtest-vs-live gap F3 exists to detect.

foundations.title: Pre-signal data pipeline hook
foundations.why: One seam both backtest and paper/live call before compute_targets. F7's data-quality gate IS this hook; F14's fallback provider and F1's eligibility plug into the same slot.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: A fallback feed (F14) bypasses the quality gate (F7) because they are bolted on separately.

foundations.title: Shared equity validation/stats module
foundations.why: metrics.py already computes Sharpe independently of forex/validation.py's validated DSR/PBO math. Promote the FX stats to a shared module instead of adding a third copy.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: Equity DSR silently uses different Sharpe math than the FX side's validated implementation.

foundations.title: Notification/telemetry channel
foundations.why: The engine runs unattended via cron; the breaker halt today only prints to a log. F12 (breaker), F9 (crowding) and F10 (promotion audit) all need to notify.

foundations.title: Run-manifest + experiment ledger
foundations.why: sweep.py results are printed, never persisted; F2's DSR needs a real n_trials count, which would otherwise be a guessed constant. F17 IS this foundation.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: F2's DSR/PBO gate reports a defensible-looking number backed by an arbitrary trial count.

foundations.title: Schema-validated state files
foundations.why: paper_state_{account}.json is one flat untyped dict. F4 (cross-currency rebalance), F10 (promotion read) and F18 (schema) all touch it. F18 IS this foundation.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: A shape change in one consumer silently breaks the other; F10 trusts a corrupted-but-parseable file.

foundations.title: Capacity-aware sizing channel inside compute_targets
foundations.why: compute_targets has no liquidity/cost input; trimming weights AFTER it in each caller recreates a second weight path. This is the single biggest invariant-#3 risk in the backlog.
foundations.risk_if_skipped: Paper and backtest apply the ADV cap differently because it lives outside the one shared function.

prerequisite_refactors.title: Unify the two cost paths into one fees.py entrypoint
prerequisite_refactors.why: backtest uses round_trip_cost_rate (turnover x rate); paper_trade inlines slippage into fill price then calls commission/stamp separately. F6 would add a third divergent path and make its own AC1 regression claim unstateable. Realizes foundation P0-C.

prerequisite_refactors.title: Capture ib_insync fill/Trade results in execution_ibkr.rebalance()
prerequisite_refactors.why: rebalance() calls ib.placeOrder() and discards the result today; three features need realized fills and would each invent a different capture schema. Realizes foundation P0-B.

prerequisite_refactors.title: Centralize ADV/volume ingestion in data.py
prerequisite_refactors.why: Both F6 (impact) and F15 (ADV cap) need trailing volume that data.py doesn't fetch; building it twice yields divergent lookback/adjustment conventions. Realizes foundation P0-A.

prerequisite_refactors.title: Strengthen test_consistency.py beyond string-grep
prerequisite_refactors.why: The current guard only checks source text ('compute_targets' present, 'select_portfolio' absent); a second sizing path could satisfy the grep while still bypassing compute_targets. Add numeric golden-fixture equality (backtest vs paper weights/costs bit-identical).

phases.name: Foundations & safety rails
phases.goal: Build the platform primitives and safety nets that carry no capital risk and unblock everything else, before anything touches sizing, cost, or live paths.
phases.exit_criteria: Data-quality gate live and default-on; CI backtest baseline green over 5 stable runs; every run emits a schema-valid manifest; state files validated on load with a migration path. Refactors R1/R2/R3/R4 landed or ticketed just-in-time before their Phase-3/4 consumers.

phases.name: Statistical validity
phases.goal: Certify the strategy's numbers honestly using the shared stats module, so no curve-fit parameter set reaches the book. Pure code, unblocked, runs in parallel with data procurement.
phases.exit_criteria: Purged/embargoed CV (embargo >= 21d, n_folds >= 6) feeds DSR/PBO with n_trials == real sweep grid size; every reported backtest shows DSR + PBO + a haircut Sharpe next to the raw number.

phases.name: Data integrity
phases.goal: Make the prices and the universe the numbers are computed on trustworthy; re-run Phase-1 certification once real point-in-time data lands.
phases.exit_criteria: run_backtest --point-in-time on real membership files for all 3 regions with >=99% reconciliation and a printed static-vs-PIT CAGR delta; delisted terminal returns represented; a feed 403 falls back through the F7 gate.

phases.name: Execution realism & capacity
phases.goal: Model real-world cost and liquidity, and instrument fills — all routed through the unified cost path and the compute_targets capacity hook so invariant #3 holds.
phases.exit_criteria: Impact-model-off reproduces today's flat-cost numbers byte-for-byte; ADV cap applied identically in backtest and paper; per-region realized-vs-modelled slippage reported; breaker alerts once per transition; crowding monitor proven read-only.

phases.name: Live readiness & reporting
phases.goal: Close the loop to real capital: measure live-vs-backtest, gate promotion, and complete portfolio/reporting. F3+F10 are the sole hard exit criteria for live capital; F4/F5 ship on their own schedule.
phases.exit_criteria: Attribution golden-run proves paper weights == backtest weights bit-for-bit; promotion gate hard-blocks live orders unless track-record + DSR/PBO + drawdown + schema-valid state criteria all pass; 0 unguarded live orders.

feature_plan.note: This item IS foundation P0-D; build first, default-on. One validator called identically by backtest and paper, never two implementations.
CI script + committed synthetic baseline JSON; early guardrail alongside F7, complements test_consistency.py.
This item IS foundation P0-G; every backtest/paper/sweep run logs into it immediately, becoming F2's honest trial-count source.
This item IS foundation P0-H; land before F4/F10 both mutate paper_state. F10 must call its validator inside the gate, not just run it in parallel.
forex/walkforward.py is shaped for (X,y) ML matrices, not sweep.py's parameter grid; write a thin equity fold generator reusing only the purge/embargo math, not walk_forward_predict itself.
validation.py port is trivial, but AC2's trial-count honesty is hollow until F8 supplies real fold structure and F17 supplies the real n_trials.
Trivial derived column on report.py; computes nothing until F2's DSR exists.
Mechanism already built (constituents.py); blocker is real vendor membership/delisted data, not code. Wire eligibility through the pre-signal hook.
Small addition to the constituents/backtest join; strictly gated on F1's real membership data landing first.
Secondary loader behind the existing interface; plugs into F7's pre-signal hook as an alternate source, never a separate path outside the gate.
Split: ship cost-unification + impact now; DEFER the borrow-cost half until a short-book epic exists (no short book today - YAGNI). Needs R1 + R3 first or it forks a third cost path.
Must extend compute_targets's signature (P0-I), not trim weights in each caller - the single biggest invariant #3 risk. Shares ADV data (R3) with F6; R4 makes the guard real.
This item IS foundation P0-B / refactor R2; build the shared fill-capture once and have F3/F10 consume it.
Pure telemetry on the existing breaker signal; easy parallel win once the shared notification channel exists.
Pure observability; ship with an explicit compute_targets-output-unchanged test (R4) so it can never quietly become a sizing input.
Must consume paper's logged actual weights/asof, never recompute with hindsight; needs fill capture (R2) for the live comparison and the ledger (P0-G) for the baseline.

Gate code is trivial (execution_ibkr hard-block); meaningless until F2 (real DSR/PBO), F3 (real tracking error) and F18 (trustworthy state) exist. Reuse the shared whole-share rounding for the min-size check (invariant #4).
Port portfolio_backtest.py's true-up-to-target into paper_trade.py via fx.py; transfers stay in the portfolio layer, charge the spread, and are gated off during a halt.
Small, reuses report.py; lowest value in the backlog (algo_trader: no) - fine to slip out of the live-readiness critical path and ship independently.

invariant_guards.how_kept: Cost / ADV-cap / quality-exclusion logic lives inside compute_targets (P0-I) or the single fees.py entrypoint (P0-C); observability items (F9) are provably read-only.
invariant_guards.test: R4: extend test_consistency.py from string-grep to numeric golden-fixture equality (backtest vs paper weights & costs bit-identical) + a sizing-neutrality test for F9's imports.

invariant_guards.how_kept: PIT membership/delisting reveal info only at the effective date; data-quality repairs use trailing data only; attribution replays the SAME asof/eligible set paper saw, with no hindsight refetch.
invariant_guards.test: members_asof boundary tests; embargo >= 21d and >= max(momentum, MA) lookback; attribution golden-run consumes the logged eligible-set.

invariant_guards.how_kept: FX spread charged on every cross-sleeve transfer; the unified cost function always fires regardless of the impact-model flag.
invariant_guards.test: Transfer-conservation test (equity conserved minus spread); F6 AC1 regression: impact-disabled output byte-identical to today's flat-cost numbers.

invariant_guards.how_kept: Sleeve accounting/attribution stays in local currency until the existing fx.py layer converts; cross-border transfers move cash only, never blend currencies mid-sleeve.
invariant_guards.test: Currency-label assertions on attribution buckets; sleeve local-currency return series unchanged by transfers except an explicit ledger entry.

invariant_guards.how_kept: The promotion gate reuses paper_trade's existing whole-share/commission-floor rounding for its min-account-size check; no parallel rounding logic.
invariant_guards.test: rebalance() raises at LIVE_PORT when equity is below N x commission floor computed via the shared whole-share path.

invariant_guards.how_kept: Every synthetic run keeps the SYNTHETIC banner; the CI baseline (F16) and DSR/PBO (F2) never surface as live performance; F5/F19 always show the disclaimer.
invariant_guards.test: Banner-presence assertion on report/tearsheet output; CI baseline JSON explicitly labelled synthetic-only.

sustainability_risks.risk: test_consistency.py only greps source text, so a second sizing path (e.g. an F15 cap applied post-hoc in paper_trade) could satisfy the guard while still bypassing compute_targets.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: R4: add numeric golden-fixture equality tests alongside the string checks, before F3/F9/F15.

sustainability_risks.risk: Two cost mechanics already diverge (backtest turnover*rate vs paper price-adjusted-fill + fees); F6 layered on top without unifying makes AC1's regression claim unstateable for both engines.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Land R1 (single fees.py entrypoint) in Phase 0, not inside F6.

sustainability_risks.risk: execution_ibkr.py discards placeOrder's result; if F11, F3 and F10 each add fill parsing independently, three Trade/fill schemas and AUD conventions emerge.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Build R2 (shared fill-capture) once, ahead of F11, consumed by F3/F10.

sustainability_risks.risk: compute_targets has a closed signature with no liquidity/cost channel; an ADV cap added outside it in each caller reopens invariant #3.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Foundation P0-I: extend the signature once and gate it with the strengthened test_consistency.py.

sustainability_risks.risk: data.py caches parquet by ticker+date only; adding a Volume column for ADV will not invalidate old Close-only caches, silently serving volume-less history.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Version the cache key (schema flag) when the ADV foundation (P0-A / R3) lands.

sustainability_risks.risk: MIN_TRADE_VALUE (execution_ibkr.py) and the micro threshold (paper_trade.py) are hardcoded module constants, not per-Region, and are already currency-unit-mismatched across AUD/USD/GBP.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Move them to Region fields before F6/F11/F15 add more thresholds, or a 4th region silently misbehaves - protects the 'add a region is one entry' property.

sustainability_risks.risk: metrics.py and forex/validation.py already compute Sharpe independently; F2/F19 risk creating a third variant.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Foundation P0-E: promote validation.py's stats to a shared module both import.

sustainability_risks.risk: Phase 4 bundles the real gates (F3, F10) with cosmetic items (F4, F5); shipping F4/F5 first could create a false sense the phase is done while the promotion gate lags.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Track F3 + F10 as the sole hard exit criteria for live capital; F4/F5 ship on their own schedule.

sustainability_risks.risk: F10 could trust paper_state track-record numbers even if F18's schema validation is only a parallel Phase-0 feature, letting a corrupted-but-parseable file pass the gate.
sustainability_risks.mitigation: Call F18's validator explicitly inside F10's gate check, not just alongside it.
